title: camissonia claviformis subsp. aurantiaca
intro: Camissonia claviformis subsp. aurantiaca, also known as the apricot mallow or apricot evening primrose, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. Camissonia claviformis subsp. aurantiaca can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its dried flowers can be used in various herbal remedies.
